rationale: REFUSE is the right single-gate-home design and the work decomposes cleanly into three beads: orphan/advisory refusal, durable-obligation check, and docs/ADR/no-regression. The execution ownership addition is minimal and correctly groups lifecycle's bead/git/branch plumbing. But OQ2 is unsound: FindOrphanedClosedBeads suppresses bd and ancestry errors, and R3 cannot compensate for an ordinary unresolved RC because pending metadata exists only after a refutation was applied. A transient detector failure followed by a successful FinalizeEpic ancestry check can therefore still auto-merge the raw-closed branch. R3 also silently loses coverage when readPlanBeadIDs fails, because ApproveImpl currently ignores planErr. OQ3 knowingly emits a recovery that fails for the branchless state, and the claimed h4n5 issue is absent from the pinned JSONL. Finally AC9 names a nonexistent plugin skill path and does not pin the two existing, non-identical skill copies. These are falsifiability/security gaps, not implementation details.
concrete_changes_required: Make impl-approve orphan detection fail closed on bd/list/ancestry errors (for example, add a strict error-returning lifecycle API while preserving best-effort wrappers for next/doctor/complete), and add error-path ACs proving FinalizeEpic and all mutations remain uncalled.

Specify authoritative R3 bead enumeration and its error posture: fail closed when plan bead IDs cannot be read, or enumerate lifecycle children from the epic per ADR-0023. Add missing/corrupt-plan tests with a branchless unsettled obligation.

Make OQ3 recovery truthful and runnable: either reconcile branchless already-closed beads in scope, or specify/test the safe manual branch-restoration/obligation-settlement procedure before complete. Commit evidence for the deferred h4n5 issue if it remains the scope justification.

Correct AC9's skill paths and enumerate all intended copies. The pinned tree has only .agents/skills/ms-impl-approve/SKILL.md and .claude/skills/ms-impl-approve/SKILL.md, they are not identical, and plugins/mindspec/skills/ms-impl-approve/SKILL.md does not exist; add runnable parity/content proofs for the chosen sources.

findings.title: OQ2 fail-open detection preserves the silent auto-merge bypass
findings.evidence: internal/lifecycle/orphans.go:71-73,75-83,101-103 converts epic lookup, closed-child listing, and ancestry failures into an empty result. internal/executor/mindspec_executor.go:394-402 independently retries ancestry and merges when that later probe succeeds. R3 only examines refutation_pending_entries, which internal/complete/panel_advisory.go:244-256 creates after an applied refutation; an unresolved RC that simply blocked has no such marker. Thus detector error -> empty R1 -> no R3 marker -> later successful FinalizeEpic probe -> ungated merge remains possible.
findings.required_change: Use a strict, error-returning detection path for the merge-terminal impl-approve consumer and test each infrastructure failure as a pre-mutation refusal. The existing best-effort API may remain for advisory/non-terminal consumers.

findings.title: R3 coverage disappears when plan.md cannot be parsed
findings.evidence: internal/approve/impl.go:225-242 calls readPlanBeadIDs but deliberately continues when planErr != nil; impl.go:452-472 returns errors for missing files/frontmatter/bead_ids. R3 says it checks every plan bead using this same list but neither the requirement nor AC6 defines a fail-closed enumeration error. A branchless/manually-merged bead with an unsettled marker is then never passed to GetMetadata and impl approve may finalize.
findings.required_change: Define and test fail-closed enumeration, preferably from authoritative epic lifecycle children or by refusing any plan read/parse error before mutation.

findings.title: OQ3's advertised recovery is knowingly non-convergent
findings.evidence: The spec admits a branchless bead fails complete at complete.go:492-495 before reconciliation at :558-572, yet R3 still mandates final recovery 'mindspec complete <bead>' and R4 calls complete the converging recourse. That is not a genuine ADR-0035 recovery without an explicit prerequisite. At pinned a0a06943, git show a0a06943:.beads/issues.jsonl contains o4fd and blp6 but no mindspec-h4n5; live bd verification was unavailable because the sandbox could not open the Dolt LOCK.
findings.required_change: State and test a safe, actionable manual prerequisite or bring branchless reconciliation into scope; ensure the deferred issue is durably present in the pinned artifact.

findings.title: AC9 is not grounded in the pinned skill tree
findings.evidence: plugins/mindspec/skills/ms-impl-approve/SKILL.md does not exist at a0a06943. The actual files are .agents/skills/ms-impl-approve/SKILL.md and .claude/skills/ms-impl-approve/SKILL.md, and cmp reports they are not byte-identical. AC9 greps only the nonexistent plugin path, while Scope says 'and mirrored copies' without enumerating or proving them.
findings.required_change: Choose and name the canonical/generated skill locations, explicitly say whether a new plugin skill is created, update every intended copy, and make AC9 verify each copy or its required parity.
